quiz.question:
Black plays Na5. Is material equal? Which side has more space? 

The kings are both safe. 

White has pressure on d6 meanwhile Black has pressure on c4. This is a matter of whose pressure is more significant. 

Based on activity, who's better?

feedback.text: After White plays e5 with a tempo on d6, Black's position crumbles. +2.4 is closer to the truth.
An evaluation of -2.5 exaggerates the value of the c4 pawn, which is doubled. Yes, Black will win that pawn. But White also has a few aces up their sleeve.
This would be true, if the only thing happening were a trade of pawns. Black wins c4 and White wins d6. But is that all that can happen?

A slight edge implies that White's position is slightly more pleasant. Maybe one piece is better, or there's a slight space advantage, but nothing major. Is that the case here, though? Remember, it's White to move!
